summary: DeVault investigates FUTO, an organization claiming to fund open source projects, and finds serious problems on multiple fronts. He discovers that FUTO's 'grant program' consists of small, unsolicited donations made through individual channels like GitHub Sponsors, after which FUTO displays project logos on their website as if they were endorsed grant recipients. Maintainers of musl libc, ffmpeg, and other listed projects were unaware of any grant relationship. Beyond the misleading grants, DeVault documents FUTO's connections to Curtis Yarvin, a self-proclaimed fascist, who was platformed on FUTO's channels. He argues that FUTO's claim of being 'apolitical' is contradicted by their many political positions on open source licensing, OSI, and their choice to promote fascist-adjacent figures.
key_points: FUTO's 'grant program' appears to consist of modest one-time donations made without following projects' established institutional sponsorship processes, then displayed as endorsements
Maintainers of musl libc, ffmpeg, and other prominent projects listed on FUTO's website were unaware of any grant and did not authorize use of their logos
FUTO founder Eron Wolf facilitated platforming Curtis Yarvin, a self-proclaimed fascist, on FUTO's media channels and in a staged 'debate' with Louis Rossmann
FUTO's claim of being apolitical is contradicted by their political positions on open source licensing, OSI, and their selective funding priorities
After the post, FUTO updated their website to clarify grants vs. donations but refused to address their platforming of Yarvin
money_quotes: The extent of the FUTO 'grant program', at least in the case of musl libc, involved ignoring musl's established process for institutional sponsors, quietly sending a modest one-time donation to one maintainer, and then plastering the logo of a well-respected open source project on a list of 'grant recipients' on their home page.
Don't argue with fascists, Louis – they aren't arguing with you, they are pitching their ideas to the audience.
It's a private, for-profit company that associates with and uses their brand to promote fascists.

There is an outrageous moral, intellectual, and political contradiction in claiming that it is apolitical to promote a person whose political program is to dismantle democracy and eject people he disagrees with from the political sphere entirely.
Smart fascists are experts at misdirection and bad-faith debate tactics and as a consequence Rossmann just becomes a vehicle for fascist propaganda.

They push an open-washing narrative and they portray themselves as a grant-making institution when, in truth, they're passing off a handful of small donations as if they were endorsements from dozens of respectable, high-profile open source projects.
key_insight: FUTO launders credibility by claiming unsolicited small donations as endorsed grants from respected FOSS projects, while using that manufactured legitimacy to platform fascist figures.
